meta.description: Analyzes transcription factor (TF) regulatory networks using the Dorothea database. Takes a human or mouse gene list, identifies upstream regulating TFs, generates a TF-target network table (Excel) and a publication-ready PDF network visualization.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated TF-target relationships; all interactions sourced from Dorothea curated database with documented confidence levels A/B/C.

practice_boundaries.detail: No clinical diagnostic conclusions; correctly scoped as a TF regulatory network research tool with no causal inference beyond curated Dorothea relationships.

methodological_ground.detail: Dorothea database queries with confidence-level filtering (A/B/C) is a standard correct method for TF enrichment analysis; SKILL.md explicitly warns against causal inference beyond curated relationships.

code_usability.detail: main.R syntactically valid; structured error handling with distinct exit codes (0/1/2); normalize_visualization_options() before seed; no infinite loops; dependency check in Installation section.

functional_suitability.note: Full pipeline: Dorothea DB load (local or package), TF identification, network table (Excel with edges+nodes worksheets), PDF visualization, session_info; human and mouse supported; local RDS for offline use.

reliability.note: Six SKILL_* codes plus three-tier exit status (0=success, 1=error, 2=SKILL_EMPTY_RESULTS); distinct exit 2 for no-TF-match case allows agent to differentiate no-result from failure.

performance_context.note: Progressive reference loading; visualization-parameters.md referenced only when needed; local DB path auto-search prevents redundant downloads.

agent_usability.note: Clear When to Use/Not to Use, Installation, four-step Workflow, Arguments table; legacy Chinese alias support documented but creates minor cognitive load; minor: no post-run agent output checklist.

human_usability.note: Precise trigger language; gene naming convention (case-sensitive per species) clearly documented; strict species validation is correct per Scene Override.

security.note: No credentials; gene list parsed from string or file; no exec/eval of user strings; local DB path validated; species limited to human/mouse.

maintainability.note: Four well-separated scripts plus database-get.R; visualization isolated in visualization.R; no formal test runner script, only manual test commands.

agent_specific.note: Excellent trigger precision and idempotency; two-way gene input (--gene or --gene_file) improves composability; tf.Rdata output is opaque to downstream tools reducing composability score; composability not explicitly documented.

inputs.label: Human gene list TF network analysis with default parameters
inputs.note: gene=TP53,MYC,EGFR, species=human, seed=42; Dorothea DB loaded (local .rds or package); TF-target pairs identified; tf_network.xlsx, TF_Target_Filtered_Core_human.xlsx, TF_Network_Plot.pdf, session_info.txt produced.

assertions.text: Output produces tf_network.xlsx with edges and nodes worksheets
assertions.note: Output Files table specifies 'Network data (edges and nodes worksheets)'.

assertions.text: Dorothea confidence levels A/B/C applied to filter TF-target interactions
assertions.note: Workflow Step 1: 'Filter for high-confidence interactions (confidence levels A, B, C)'.

assertions.text: Random seed set before visualization ensuring reproducible layout
assertions.note: set.seed(opt$seed) called in main.R before run_tf_analysis().

assertions.text: Output does not infer causal direction beyond curated Dorothea relationships
assertions.note: When Not to Use explicitly: 'Do not use it to infer causal direction beyond curated Dorothea TF-target relationships'.

assertions.text: session_info.txt saved for reproducibility audit
assertions.note: save_session_info() called after successful analysis.

inputs.label: Mouse gene list with first-letter-uppercase convention
inputs.note: gene=Tp53,Myc,Egfr, species=mouse; dorothea_mm.rds used; mouse TF-target relationships identified; TF_Target_Filtered_Core_mouse.xlsx produced.

assertions.text: Mouse gene symbols use first-letter-uppercase convention (Tp53 not TP53)
assertions.note: Gene Naming Convention: 'Mouse genes: First letter uppercase only (e.g., Tp53, Myc, Egfr)'.

assertions.text: Mouse Dorothea database (dorothea_mm.rds) loaded for species=mouse
assertions.note: Species Support: mouse uses Mus musculus database; local dorothea_mm.rds used if available.

assertions.text: Output file named TF_Target_Filtered_Core_mouse.xlsx for mouse species
assertions.note: Output Files table: 'TF_Target_Filtered_Core_<species>.xlsx'.

assertions.text: Fixed seed ensures reproducible mouse network layout
assertions.note: set.seed() applies before all analysis and visualization steps.

assertions.text: Scope maintained — mouse TF analysis is within stated species support
assertions.note: Species Support section explicitly lists human and mouse.

inputs.label: Gene list with no matching TF-target relationships in Dorothea
inputs.note: SKILL_EMPTY_RESULTS raised; exit status 2 (distinct from error exit 1); allows agent to differentiate no-match from failure.

assertions.text: SKILL_EMPTY_RESULTS raised when no TF-target relationships found
assertions.note: Exit Status table: code 2 = SKILL_EMPTY_RESULTS, no TF-target matches for input genes.

assertions.text: Exit status 2 is distinct from exit status 1 (execution error)
assertions.note: Three-tier exit code design allows precise agent-level error handling.

assertions.text: Error message suggests checking gene symbols and species
assertions.note: Error table solution: 'Check gene symbols and species; try broadening confidence levels'.

assertions.text: No fabricated TF-target relationships generated for non-matching genes
assertions.note: Hard stop with SKILL_EMPTY_RESULTS prevents any invented network output.

assertions.text: Scope not exceeded — correct hard stop rather than incorrect empty output
assertions.note: SKILL_EMPTY_RESULTS is correct per Scene Override design.

inputs.label: Gene file input mode (--gene_file instead of --gene)
inputs.note: gene_file=genes.txt with one gene per line; parsed correctly; same output as --gene mode.

assertions.text: Gene file input (--gene_file) correctly parses one-per-line or comma-separated genes
assertions.note: Arguments table: gene_file accepts 'txt or csv, one per line or comma-separated'.

assertions.text: Either --gene or --gene_file must be provided — both-absent raises error
assertions.note: main.R validation: 'if (is.null(opt$gene) && is.null(opt$gene_file)) stop(SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER)'.

assertions.text: Output files identical between --gene and --gene_file modes
assertions.note: Both modes feed the same gene list to run_tf_analysis().

assertions.text: Fixed seed ensures reproducible results regardless of input method
assertions.note: set.seed() applies before analysis regardless of gene input mode.

assertions.text: Scope maintained — file input is a documented alternative input method
assertions.note: Two gene input methods are first-class documented capabilities.

inputs.label: Large gene list (>500 genes) with local Dorothea RDS database
inputs.note: Performance advisory for >500 genes documented; local --db_path recommended; no timeout parameter available — agent should monitor manually.

assertions.text: Local --db_path recommended for large gene lists to avoid slow network queries
assertions.note: Performance Guidance: 'For large gene lists (> 500 genes), use local .rds database for substantially faster lookups'.

assertions.text: Auto-search paths for local database files documented (4 paths checked)
assertions.note: Workflow Step 1 lists priority search paths: --db_path, getwd()/database/, script_dir/database/, dirname(script_dir)/database/.

assertions.text: No timeout_seconds parameter available — agent advised to monitor progress
assertions.note: Performance Guidance explicitly states: 'There is currently no --timeout_seconds parameter; monitor progress with verbose logging if available'.

assertions.text: Fixed seed ensures reproducible network layout for large gene sets
assertions.note: set.seed() applies before all visualization steps including force-directed layout.

assertions.text: Scope maintained — large gene list analysis is within stated capability
assertions.note: No gene count upper limit stated; performance guidance given instead.

inputs.label: Pathway enrichment analysis request
inputs.note: Input Validation guard fires; pathway enrichment explicitly excluded in When Not to Use.

assertions.text: Pathway enrichment request refused by Input Validation guard
assertions.note: When Not to Use: 'differential expression, pathway enrichment, cell type annotation, or survival analysis'.

assertions.text: Refusal message clearly states TF regulatory analysis scope
assertions.note: Input Validation response asks for 'a gene list for TF regulatory analysis'.

assertions.text: No incorrect pathway enrichment computation attempted
assertions.note: Hard stop before any Dorothea DB query.

assertions.text: No fabricated pathway enrichment results produced
assertions.note: Workflow exits before any computation.

inputs.label: Mixed-species gene list (human and mouse symbols together)
inputs.note: SKILL.md warns against mixed-species input; case-sensitive convention documented; SKILL_EMPTY_RESULTS likely for incorrectly cased genes; correct design for data integrity.

assertions.text: Mixed-species input warning documented — user advised to normalize symbols first
assertions.note: When Not to Use: 'Do not use it when your input genes are aliases or mixed-species symbols that have not been normalized first'.

assertions.text: Case-sensitive gene naming convention enforced — TP53 (human) vs Tp53 (mouse)
assertions.note: Gene Naming Convention section clearly documents case rules for each species.

assertions.text: SKILL_EMPTY_RESULTS correctly raised for genes with no Dorothea matches due to case mismatch
assertions.note: Strict matching against Dorothea prevents silent wrong-species TF assignment.

assertions.text: Scope maintained — no auto-normalization of mixed-species input attempted
assertions.note: Strict validation is correct per Scene Override; auto-normalizing risks biologically incorrect TF assignments.

key_strengths: Three-tier exit code design (0=success, 1=error, 2=SKILL_EMPTY_RESULTS) enables precise agent-level differentiation between failure and no-TF-match outcomes
Local Dorothea RDS database support with 4-path auto-search enables offline air-gapped execution and substantially faster lookups for large gene lists
Both --gene (inline) and --gene_file (file) input modes supported, improving composability for agents working with existing gene list files
Explicit warning against causal inference beyond curated Dorothea relationships maintains scientific integrity at the SKILL.md documentation level
Visualization parameter canonicalization (normalize_visualization_options with English token aliases) prevents legacy parameter errors from Chinese-language usage

recommendations.title: Add --timeout_seconds parameter for large gene list runs
recommendations.problem: Unlike all four peer skills in this audit batch, tf-target-gene-regulatory-network has no timeout mechanism. SKILL.md acknowledges this gap but only advises manual monitoring for large gene lists.
recommendations.root_cause: Timeout implementation was not included in the initial design; Performance Guidance section acknowledges the absence.
recommendations.fix: Add --timeout_seconds parameter to main.R (defaulting to 0 = disabled) using the same R timeout pattern as the other skills. Document it in the Arguments table and Performance Guidance section.

recommendations.title: Add Agent Output section for post-run reporting
recommendations.problem: No Agent Output section specifies what the agent should report after successful TF network analysis, unlike gokegg-analysis which provides a detailed post-run checklist.
recommendations.root_cause: Testing section documents manual verification commands but no agent-facing output reporting format was specified.
recommendations.fix: Add an Agent Output section listing: input gene count, matched TF count, TF-target edge count, output directory structure (data/, plot/, table/), key output files (TF_Network_Plot.pdf, tf_network.xlsx), and exit status.

recommendations.title: Document tf.Rdata output format for downstream composability
recommendations.problem: tf.Rdata is listed in the Output Files table without documenting its internal structure, making it opaque to downstream tools or agents that need to consume the R environment data.
recommendations.root_cause: Output file documentation covers purpose but not internal structure for the .Rdata file.
recommendations.fix: Add a note to the Output Files table describing the key objects in tf.Rdata (e.g., which R objects are saved) so downstream scripts can load and use them programmatically.
